questions.question: In photography, "framing" as a composition technique means…
options: Using elements within the scene to surround and draw attention to the subject
Adding a printed border to the final image
Locking the exposure before you recompose
Choosing the file format before shooting
questions.reviewHint: Framing uses elements in the scene itself to create a 'frame within the frame' around the subject.

questions.question: Which of these is a classic example of a natural frame?
options: Overhanging tree branches around the subject
questions.reviewHint: Natural elements like branches, foliage or rock openings make effective frames.

questions.question: An archway or doorway used to frame a subject is an example of…
questions.reviewHint: Architectural structures - arches, doorways, windows - are commonly used as frames.

questions.question: Besides solid objects, what else can be used to frame a subject?
questions.reviewHint: Light and shadow can frame a subject just as physical elements can.

questions.question: One of the main benefits of framing is that it…
options: Adds a sense of depth to the image
Increases the dynamic range of the sensor
questions.reviewHint: Framing adds depth and a feeling of looking 'into' the scene toward the subject.

questions.question: Framing also helps a photograph by…
options: Directing the viewer's attention to the main subject
questions.reviewHint: A frame guides the eye straight to the subject it surrounds.

questions.question: When choosing a framing element, a key best practice is to…
options: Make sure it relates to the subject and doesn't overpower it
Always make it brighter than the subject
Place it only in the centre of the frame
Use the longest lens you own
questions.reviewHint: The frame should complement the subject, not compete with or distract from it.

questions.question: A risk to avoid when framing is…
options: Letting the frame become so dominant it hides or distracts from the subject
questions.reviewHint: If the framing element overpowers the subject, it works against the composition.

questions.question: Framing is especially useful for…
options: Enhancing storytelling and adding context around the subject
Fixing white balance in mixed light
questions.reviewHint: A frame can add narrative context and depth, strengthening the visual story.

questions.question: To keep the subject clear when using a frame, you should…
options: Maintain enough separation and focus so the subject stays the clear point of interest
Frame with the brightest object available
Place the subject outside the frame
questions.reviewHint: Keep the subject distinct and well-focused so the frame supports rather than competes with it.
